title: India needs dedicated AI law as current legal framework inadequate to tackle emerging risks: Cyber Expert Pavan Duggal
summary: India needs a dedicated law on artificial intelligence as existing legal frameworks are not adequate to deal with the risks emerging from AI, cyber law and artificial intelligence law expert Pavan Duggal said on the sidelines of FICCI's CyberComm event in the national capital.

title: Google rejects UK union recognition but offers talks via conciliation body
summary: Under UK rules, the move opens a 20-working-day window, extendable by agreement, for talks on recognition. If talks fail, unions ‌can seek statutory ⁠recognition ⁠via the country's independent Central Arbitration Committee.

title: Lenskart to step up AI push in FY27, targets scale-up in eye tests, automation
summary: Lenskart is pivoting to a consumer-AI company, making artificial intelligence central to its FY27 growth strategy. The company aims to integrate AI across its value chain, from eye tests and product design to manufacturing and delivery, to enhance customer acquisition and eye-testing capacity.

title: CCI okays Blackstone, other investors' proposal to acquire stake in AI firm Neysa Networks
summary: India's competition regulator CCI has approved a deal. Blackstone-backed funds and other investors will acquire a stake in Neysa Networks. This AI acceleration cloud provider recently raised $1.2 billion. Neysa is set to scale up its operations. The company plans to deploy over 20,000 GPUs in India.
